fab_section_md:
- Queen Helaena was spared by Blood and Cheese but broken by the ordeal; afterward she would not eat, bathe, or leave her chambers (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p01:15)
- She could no longer bear to look upon her son Maelor, whom she had named to die (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p01:15)
- She and Aegon slept apart thereafter and she sank into madness (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p01:15)
- She was the rider of Dreamfyre but spent her days weeping (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p01:189)
identity_line: Helaena — Queen Helaena was spared by Blood and Cheese but broken by the ordeal; afterward she would not eat, bathe, or leave her chambers.

fab_section_md:
- Marilda of Hull, called "Mouse," was a shipwright's daughter who bore Addam in 114 AC and Alyn in 115 (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p01:227)
- She became a canny trader owning seven ships by 130 AC (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p01:227)
- She long refused to name the boys' father, at last claiming the late Ser Laenor (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p01:233)
identity_line: Marilda of Hull, called "Mouse," was a shipwright's daughter who bore Addam in 114 AC and Alyn in 115.

fab_section_md: - Munkun names her Nettles; Mushroom calls her Netty, a dockside whore's bastard (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p01:249)
identity_line: Nettles — Munkun names her Nettles; Mushroom calls her Netty, a dockside whore's bastard.

fab_section_md: - Lord of Rook's Rest, who defied Criston and sent a raven to Dragonstone pleading for succor (fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p01:119)
identity_line: Lord Staunton — Lord of Rook's Rest, who defied Criston and sent a raven to Dragonstone pleading for succor.
